articles.title: Burnham will be bolder but stick to Labour manifesto, deputy leader says
articles.summary: Meanwhile Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch says Burnham is a "people pleaser", ahead of him becoming prime minister on Monday.

articles.title: Messi on the brink of history - will it be his last World Cup game?
articles.summary: Argentina captain Lionel Messi will play his 34th World Cup match in Sunday's final, but will it be his last appearance?

articles.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
articles.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

articles.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
articles.summary: Five people are killed and at least 35 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

articles.title: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
articles.summary: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

articles.title: Record numbers of honours for working class heroes
articles.summary: Record numbers of people from poorer backgrounds are now receiving awards.

articles.title: The 1975's Matty Healy marries model Gabbriette Bechtel in Los Angeles
articles.summary: The newlyweds were first linked in 2023 after Healy's brief relationship with Taylor Swift.

articles.summary: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

articles.title: Why the half-time show may be a taste of things to come
articles.summary: The 2026 World Cup's spectacle is creating debate about the place of music in sport - and how it could infiltrate other tournaments.

articles.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
articles.summary: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

articles.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
articles.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

articles.title: Can Burnham's casual style survive contact with No 10?
articles.summary: When Burnham becomes PM, he'll find it harder to wear the wardrobe that's central to his political brand.

articles.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
articles.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

articles.title: Could there be a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
articles.summary: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.

articles.title: Digital ID plan to be scrapped in Burnham's first major policy pledge
articles.summary: A spokesperson for the incoming prime minister says his government would “put its focus where people need it right now”.

articles.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
articles.summary: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia

articles.title: Chinese firm seeks compensation over British Steel nationalisation
articles.summary: Jingye said it will take action "through legal means to the very end" after the UK government nationalised British Steel.

articles.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
articles.summary: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

articles.title: Britain 'set for bumper year' for butterflies
articles.summary: A hot and dry spring means a greater variety and more butterflies across the UK, a charity says.

articles.title: Minnesota senior wins law change so she can enjoy Happy Hour at care home
articles.summary: Anita Le Brun, the face of a campaign to allow for alcohol to be served at care homes without a licence, is celebrating its success.

articles.title: 'I had stage four cancer at 14, now I'm a doctor'
articles.summary: Ellie Waters-Barnes survived an aggressive stage four cancer and underwent 18 months of treatment.

articles.title: Escaped big cat found in plant pot after two-month search
articles.summary: It is thought to have escaped from the city and travelled about four miles into the countryside.

articles.summary: Hollywood star fulfils promise of Moana screening for children at St Gregory Primary School.

articles.title: Patience, power, perfection - Duckett's record-breaking innings
articles.summary: Ben Duckett rewrites the record books as he scores 141 in the third and final One Day International against India, the highest score by any men's batter in an ODI at Lord's, beating the previous record of 138, set by Sir Viv Richards in 1975.
